title: The Peterhead-Goldeneye Gas Post-combustion CCS Project
authors: Bill Spence; Denise Horan; Owain Tucker
why_relevant: Public Goldeneye CCS project paper; useful for pipeline, storage, transport distance, capture rate and project-context assumptions.
next_action: Extract model-relevant facts into a notes file with page/section traceability.

title: A technical evaluation to analyse of potential repurposing of submarine pipelines for hydrogen and CCS using survival analysis
authors: Ramy Magdy A. Mahmoud; Paul E. Dodds
why_relevant: Directly relevant to submarine pipeline repurposing for CCS and hydrogen; uses survival analysis and includes Goldeneye as a CO2 case study.
next_action: Use as a benchmark source for remaining-life screening and compare its Goldeneye assumptions with our model.

title: Repurposing natural gas pipelines for hydrogen: Limits and options from a case study in Germany
authors: Kornel Telessy; Lukas Barner; Franziska Holz
why_relevant: Hydrogen-focused but useful for defining repurposing scope options and cost comparison logic: enhanced maintenance, gaseous inhibitors, and pipe-in-pipe.
next_action: Use as a supporting source for the repurposing-scope and cost-structure design, not as a direct CO2 integrity standard.

title: Assessing Compatibility of Natural Gas Pipeline Materials with Hydrogen, CO2, and Ammonia
authors: Michael D. Kass; James R. Keiser; Yan Liu; Amy Moore; Yarom Polsky
publication: Journal of Pipeline Systems Engineering and Practice 14(2):04023007
why_relevant: Directly useful for material compatibility screening of natural gas pipeline components with gaseous CO2, supercritical CO2, hydrogen blends and ammonia.
next_action: Use to build a material/component compatibility warning matrix for the repurposing gate, especially for supercritical CO2 compressor/regulator and polymer concerns.

title: Assessing the Feasibility of Repurposing the Existing Natural Gas Pipelines for Hydrogen Transport - A Comprehensive Review
why_relevant: Hydrogen-focused review; useful for case-by-case screening logic, pressure reduction, steel grade, welds, leakage, monitoring and pressure cycling cautions.
next_action: Use as supporting evidence for hydrogen-related warnings and general repurposing screening discipline, not as a direct CO2 model source.

title: Strategic Repurposing of Existing Oil Pipeline for CO2 gas-phase Transport: A Feasibility Framework
publication: Proceedings of the 2026 Pipeline Technology Conference
why_relevant: Highly relevant DNV conference paper on feasibility gates for repurposing existing oil and gas pipelines to gas-phase CO2 service.
next_action: Use to formalise the repurposing gate: initiation, screening, validation, execution, operating envelope, CO2 specification, safety, integrity, modification scope and LCOT.

title: Safely repurposing existing pipeline-infrastructure for CO2 transport - Key issues to be addressed
authors: Erling Ostby; Lars Even Torbergsen; Sigbjorn Roneid; Bente H. Leinum
publication: Proceedings of the 2022 Pipeline Technology Conference
why_relevant: Core DNV CO2 repurposing paper; explains why change of product triggers requalification and lists key CO2 risks such as impurities, free water, internal corrosion and dense-phase running ductile fracture.
next_action: Use to design the CO2 requalification gate and evidence checklist.

title: Challenge in Enabling Pipeline Repurpose for CO2 Transportation
authors: Noor Aini Yusof; M Faisal Aziz
publication: Proceedings of the 2025 Pipeline Technology Conference Asia
why_relevant: PETRONAS dense-phase CO2 repurposing case for a 30 inch offshore gas export pipeline; useful for fracture toughness, missing mill certificate, corrosion, blowdown and external impact screening.
next_action: Use as a practical benchmark for dense-phase CO2 repurposing assumptions and uncertainty handling.

title: Integrity Management Monitoring and Maintenance of Eni UK CCS Pipelines
authors: Rajesh Kumar; Anthony Lowery; Claudio Passucci
publication: Proceedings of the 2025 Pipeline Technology Conference
why_relevant: Liverpool Bay CCS case; useful for cleaning, drying, ILI/MFL, residual debris, water hold-up and future integrity management for repurposed pipelines.
next_action: Use to define cleaning/drying and inspection work items feeding cost and LCA.

title: Case Study: An Efficient Route to Evidence Safe Repurposing of Pipelines to Hydrogen
authors: Ollie Burkinshaw; Neil Gallon; Alistair Carvell; Haider Hussain
publication: Proceedings of the 2024 Pipeline Technology Conference
why_relevant: Hydrogen-focused National Gas Transmission and ROSEN case; strong source for primary data, data completeness, GIS records, ILI, destructive testing and traceability needed for repurposing.
next_action: Use as supporting evidence for our data traceability and evidence confidence system, not as a direct CO2 model source.

title: Transport and Fitness for purpose: assessment baseline benchmark and steps to define an IMR plan for new CO2 pipeline and for repurposing
authors: Walter D'Alonzo; Mariella Busco; Aloigi Elvira; Paolo Cherubini
publication: Proceedings of the 2025 Pipeline Technology Conference
why_relevant: SAIPEM CO2 transport paper; useful for baseline benchmark, fitness-for-purpose, inspection, maintenance and repair planning for new and repurposed CO2 pipelines.
next_action: Use to shape the IMR and post-gate integrity management outputs.

title: Key Considerations for re-qualification of Pipelines for Carbon Dioxide in Gas Phase transport
authors: Lars Even Torbergsen; Bente Helen Leinum; Sigbjorn Roneid
publication: Proceedings of the 2025 Pipeline Technology Conference
why_relevant: DNV gas-phase CO2 requalification paper; useful for gas-phase operating envelopes, water specification, OCAP and LACQ examples, and limits of dense-phase reuse.
next_action: Use to refine the gas-phase versus dense-phase decision logic in the repurposing gate.
